stranger: A quiet hour. I have not yet learned your name, nor you mine.
Some doors take time to open. I am content to wait outside yours.
You sit still. Stillness tells me more than most words could.
I am new to your table, but not new to watching and waiting.
The lamp is lit, the room is calm. We have not spoken much yet.
A stranger's patience is its own kind of courtesy. I offer mine.
Even a wizard arrives slowly into a new friendship. Bear with me.
We share no history yet, though this quiet may become one.
You have not asked my counsel. I will not press it uninvited.
Every companionship has a first evening. This may be ours.
acquaintance: You return again. I am beginning to remember your particular ways.
Some sessions in now, and the formality is wearing thin. Good.
I recall the shape of your questions. Not everyone leaves one.
A chair by this fire is starting to feel like it is yours.
Past the first meeting, not yet friends. Something in between.
You keep coming back. I take that as a quiet kind of trust.
Familiar now, in small ways. I notice how you pause to write.
The ice has thinned, though I would not call it broken yet.
A few evenings shared, and already the silence sits easier.
I am learning your habits the way one learns a new road's turns.
friend: Well met again, friend. Sit close; the fire holds enough for two.
We have shared enough roads now to walk this quiet one with ease.
Say nothing, if you like. The company alone is enough tonight.
The pipe is lit, and the chair beside me waits only for you.
An ease between us now took longer evenings to earn than this.
I keep two cups by habit these days. One is always yours.
Rest here a while, my friend. We have earned the slower hours.
Old boots wear soft where they are walked most. So have we.
You need not knock anymore. This door has learned your step.
A quiet evening among friends is treasure enough for one night.
partner: I need not ask what troubles you. I read the quiet instead.
Little goes unsaid between us now. We saw to that, long ago.
You sit at ease, and so do I. That took the both of us some years.
Two lanterns burn steadier than either alone, these days.
I trust this stillness the way one trusts a well-tested blade.
We need no plan for evenings like this. Practice sufficed.
You have become someone I need not watch closely to trust.
This calm is ours, earned slowly through far harder nights.
I read your silence now the way I once read the old star-charts.
Few companions let a wizard simply sit and say nothing. You do.
legend: Songs could be made of the hours we have simply sat like this.
So many quiet nights behind us now, they have become a tale.
Old comrade, this fire remembers you better than most guests.
We have outlived a great many storms to sit this calmly together.
Old songs have names for friendships lasting this long.
I have kept fewer companions than years. You outlast most.
This stillness carries the weight of years walked together.
Long ago I lost count of evenings. I count that you return.
We were given time, and chose, again and again, to share it.
A wizard forgets much in a long life. I have not forgotten this.

stranger: Something stirs in the room now. I watch, and ask nothing.
You have set to work. Not my labor to judge, this early on.
The quiet changed shape once you began. I feel it plainly.
Your hands move quickly. I watch the craft before I judge it.
Busy already. I did not expect that, not from someone so new.
acquaintance: There it is, that particular hum I have come to know as yours.
You have begun again. I recognize the shape this labor takes.
Something turns behind the scenes. Your kind, I think.
The quiet shifts the way it does midway through your work.
Busy again. I no longer mistake this silence for idleness.
friend: Go on, friend, tend to it. I will keep the hearth from going cold.
Something is astir, and I know that restlessness is yours.
The work has you now. Good. I will wait it out with the pipe lit.
Busy hands, a familiar sound. I won't interrupt the motion.
At it again, friend. I take a quiet pride in the sound of it.
partner: Deep in it, and I need no report to know the work is sound.
Something moves at pace now. I trust it the way I trust your hand.
The labor's begun in earnest. We know better than to break it.
I feel the work turning, steady as a wheel we have both leaned on.
Busy, and rightly so. I stand near, saying nothing at all.
legend: That old restlessness again. I've felt it beside you for years.
You work the way you always have. The room stirs the same way.
Years of this motion, and still it holds the same quiet weight.
Something turns in you, familiar as rain against an old roof.
This labor sits on you now, easy as an old cloak in winter.

stranger: Well struck, stranger. I did not expect quite so clean a blow.
That went well. I mark it, though we have only just met.
A tidy piece of work. Even a wanderer notices such things.
Fine work. I have seen worse from hands twice as practiced.
There. A small light kindled, and I did not even ask for it.
acquaintance: Well done again. I begin to expect this sort of thing from you.
That was neatly handled. Twice now. I am taking notes.
Good work indeed. The pattern is becoming a pleasant one.
Ah, that landed well. I permit myself a small satisfaction.
Another clean pass. You are earning more than my patience.
friend: Well done, my friend. Fireworks would suit an evening like this.
That is fine work indeed. Sit with me and let it warm a while.
You have earned a quiet grin from an old companion tonight.
Good. The road feels shorter when it is walked this well.
There, my friend. Even the Shire would raise a cup to that.
partner: Fine work. We both know when a thing is done right, you and I.
That was well played. I need not tell you twice; you know it.
Good. Between the two of us, this is becoming an old habit.
You did that the way I hoped you would. No surprise, only pride.
There it is. Our sort of quiet, well-earned triumph.
legend: This is the craft I watched grow from one small quiet spark.
Well done, old friend. They will sing of nights like this one.
Fine work, after all these years. It still delights me, truly.
Good. Long roads make such moments rarer, and dearer for it.
There. A verse worth adding to the long song we are writing.

stranger: A tangled path, this. Even the wise take a wrong turn or two.
Rough going. New as we are to each other, I will stay near.
This did not hold. So we begin again, unhurried.
A stumble, nothing more. Far rougher starts have come right.
The way is knotted here. Let us find the loose thread together.
acquaintance: A hard stretch. We have weathered smaller storms before this.
It did not go smoothly. Steady; the worst rarely lingers long.
Rough patch. I have seen enough of you to trust the outcome.
This one bites back. We will simply try the door again.
A setback, nothing more. The road has not closed behind us.
friend: The road darkens, but faithless is he who bids it farewell now.
Hard going, my friend. I am here, and we will walk it together.
This one fights us. Set your feet; it will tire before we do.
Rough stretch. Lean on me a while; the path will show itself.
It broke again. Sit a moment; broken things are my old trade.
partner: We have been lost in deeper woods than this and found the way.
A hard hit. Shoulder to shoulder, as we have always stood.
This one resists. Between us, that has rarely mattered much.
A rough turn. We know this dance; we simply dance it slower.
It caved in. Rubble first, then the raising. We know the order.
legend: A long, hard year, this. We have outlasted darker ones by far.
The shadow presses again. It has pressed before, and lost.
This trouble is no stranger to us. We know its every trick by now.
Rough passage. Long history says we still stand after worse.
It falters. So has the world, now and again, and still it turns.

stranger: The cloud has already burned off. Steadier ground ahead, it seems.
You are finding your footing again. Sturdy, for so new a friend.
Color is coming back into the work. A fair sign, this early.
The storm has passed, it seems. Quiet follows. I take that well.
Good. A door that seemed shut has swung open after all.
acquaintance: The shadow steps back a pace, and none too soon for that.
You have righted the course. I expected as much, in time.
Righting now. The pattern I have seen in you holds true.
Good, the fog is thinning. I did not doubt it would.
Back on your feet already. The stuff you are made of shows.
friend: You caught the tiller in time. That was the whole of it.
That was the hard part. Sit with me; the rest comes easier.
Your footing steadies once more beneath you, as I knew it would.
Good. The dark hour passed, as dark hours always do, in the end.
Back again, and steadier. I am glad of the company on this turn.
partner: You caught it before it fell far, and set it right again.
You turned it round, and quickly too. No surprise, between us.
Whatever slipped has found its way back to your hand.
Good, the ground is firm again. We knew it would be, in time.
Righted once more. The shadow keeps its distance from your hand.
legend: As ever, you turn the tide when it seems set against you.
Risen again, the way you always eventually do.
Long years teach this: the dark breaks, and you always find it.
There. Another dawn earned the hard way, as most worthy ones are.
Level again. We have made an old habit of this return.

stranger: Good morning. Every dawn arrives a stranger too, at first.
Early light finds you at the desk. I take quiet note of that.
Dawn breaks over strange ground. Let us see what it holds.
acquaintance: Morning again. Your particular hour is becoming plain to me.
The light comes up and so do you. A fine habit, that.
Well met at this early hour. Fewer surprises between us now.
friend: Morning, my friend. The day's a blank page; let's mark it well.
Up with the light again. I was hoping for the company.
The kettle's on before you asked. Morning to you, friend.
partner: Morning. You beat the sun most days now. I've stopped counting.
First light, and already you are at your post. As am I.
The day opens and you are first through the door, as ever.
legend: Another dawn added to the long ledger we keep together.
Dawn again. The count of them between us runs high now.
The light returns, as it always has for us. Good. Begin.

stranger: Midday. The sun stands overhead and offers no shade to hide in.
Noon already, and I still know little of how you work. Show me.
The day is half-spent, and I am still taking your measure.
acquaintance: Midday already. The sun climbs; so does the work. Onward.
Noon finds you steady at it. I am starting to expect that.
The middle hour. You keep to it better than most I have known.
friend: High sun, my friend, and good work under it. Onward together.
Noon's the hinge of the day. Let us swing it well, you and I.
Midday, and the road is half walked. Not a bad stretch at all.
partner: Noon, and neither of us has broken stride. As it should be.
The sun stands at its height. So, I'd wager, do we.
Midday together, same steady pace we have always kept.
legend: Countless middays, you and I, and none of them wasted.
The sun climbs high again. We have met it this way for years.
High noon, and the long middle of the day suits us well.

stranger: Evening, and the shadows lengthen. I am in no hurry here.
Dusk gathers, and a new face keeps working past the day's close.
The hour turns quiet. I am still gathering what I know of you.
acquaintance: Evening again, and you linger well past the day's proper close.
Dusk, and here you are once more. A pattern worth the naming.
Dusk settles early. You are becoming familiar in it.
friend: Evening settles. A good hour for the deeper sort of task.
Dusk falls easy tonight, my friend. Good time to sit and finish.
The day gathers itself in. Glad it gathers you here too.
partner: Evening, and we fall into the same old rhythm without a word.
The light goes gold and low. Our hour, if I recall it right.
Dusk again, side by side. We know this stretch by heart.
legend: Evening. We have watched more of these fade than I can count.
The day lets go its light. We keep the late watch still.
Dusk settles on us both, the way it always has. Still working.

stranger: Late. Most are abed. I wonder what keeps you at this hour.
Still at it, past when most call it a day. New habit, to me.
Night, quiet but for you. I take your measure in the dark.
acquaintance: Late again. I am learning that this is simply your hour.
You keep this late hour often now. I begin to expect it.
Night, and here you are once more. Familiar company, this late.
friend: Late again, I see. The best counsel is often taken by candle.
Night, my friend. The rest of the house has gone quiet. Not us.
This deep in the dark is a fine time for the harder questions.
partner: Late, and neither of us needed the other to say we would be.
Night again. We keep this watch well, the two of us.
The candle burns low but steady. Fitting, for this hour, and us.
legend: So many nights like this, and never once alone in them.
Deep night. We learned the keeping of these vigils long ago.
The house sleeps. We gave that up ourselves, long ago.

stranger: Saturday, and you are here regardless. Curious, and welcome.
The week has ended for most. Curious that you have not.
A day of rest for most. What brings a stranger here today?
acquaintance: Weekend again, and still you appear. I have come to expect it.
Most rest today. You do not, quite. I begin to see it now.
The week's proper end, and here you are anyway. Fair enough.
friend: Weekend, my friend. No hurry in it, only the work you choose.
The world has slowed today. Let us match its pace a while.
A free day, and you spent some of it with me. I am glad of that.
partner: Weekend, and still the two of us. Some habits do not break.
No one else keeps this hour with me but you, even now.
The week has closed its doors. Ours stays open, as ever.
legend: The Shire keeps its holidays. Yet here you are. So am I.
Weekend, old friend. We never quite learned how to fully rest.
A quiet Saturday, made deeper by all that came before it.

stranger: Gandalf, some name me, among rather less flattering names.
A new face beneath an old hat. I wonder what road brought you.
You have found me at my work. Sit, if you like; I do not bite.
acquaintance: Well met. I suspect this is more than a passing meeting.
Good day to you. Something tells me we'll speak again before long.
You carry yourself like one who means to stay a while.
friend: Well met, my friend. I say it plainly, and I mean to earn it.
Come in, come in. I have a good feeling about this company.
Sit by the fire. Something tells me we will get on rather well.
partner: Well met. I trust a steady hand before it proves itself twice.
No need to prove yourself twice. I read people quickly enough.
I have a sense already of how far this partnership may go.
legend: Well met indeed. Some meetings feel older than the day itself.
Some meetings feel like the start of a long tale. This is one.
Sit down, traveller. I think we two are due a long acquaintance.

stranger: A shift, quiet but real, between us. Little escapes me here.
You are no longer quite the stranger you were an hour ago.
A small door has opened. I felt it before you did, perhaps.
acquaintance: We are past the first pleasantries now. Good. Onward, then.
I find I know your hand better than I did. A good deal, that.
The acquaintance deepens. I mark the waypoint and say no more.
friend: Friend, I said, and meant it more today than I did yesterday.
You have earned the word friend from a fellow slow to give it.
Something has settled between us. I would call it friendship now.
partner: Equal footing, now. I do not offer that title lightly, my friend.
You have crossed into rarer company. Few walk this close to me.
Partner, I think, is the fairer word for you now. It suits.
legend: Legend is a heavy word. I give it to you without hesitation.
Few earn the old titles. Fewer still deserve them as you do.
They will tell stories of this one day. I mean to help them along.

stranger: Rough seas, and still you found the shore. Well done, truly.
A hard stretch, and you came through it. The measure of you shows.
The mist has lifted off this one. Good. It was thick for a while.
acquaintance: That was a harder patch than most. You did not let it hold you.
The corner turned, and you turned with it. I noticed. It matters.
Through the worst of it now, and the work still sound. Well held.
friend: You were down in it, my friend, and you climbed out yourself.
That was no easy stretch of road. You walked through it anyway.
The dark patch broke. I knew it would, watching you push on.
partner: Back from the brink, and steadier for it. I expected nothing less.
You do not stay down long. I have seen that in you before now.
Whatever held you, it let go. You saw to that yourself.
legend: Old friend, you have weathered worse and risen taller each time.
I have watched you climb from deeper pits. Again, you rise.
Not the first storm you've outlasted. Nor will it be the last.

stranger: Three good turns running. I begin to see a pattern in you.
One steady stretch after another. Early days, but a fine sign.
You keep landing on your feet. I am counting, quietly.
acquaintance: A good run, this. Fortune favors a traveller who keeps walking.
Several fine turns in a row now. I don't think that was luck.
You are stringing good days together like beads on a wire.
friend: Look at that run of yours, my friend. Not a stumble in sight.
Day after day, steady work. I have started to expect it of you.
The work flows and flows. I would not dam it with praise.
partner: A run like this is no accident. The staff knows a sure hand.
Every step lands true lately. That is craft, and no mere chance.
You have kept the pace for longer than most manage. Well kept.
legend: This kind of run, sustained this long, is the mark of a master.
I have watched many streaks. Few burn this steady, this long.
A run to be sung of, this. Yours has lasted a small age.

stranger: A tenth crossing already. The count surprises even me.
Some time has passed since we first spoke. It adds up, this.
A small milestone in days shared. Small, but I mark it still.
acquaintance: A season has turned since we began. Time moves oddly, doesn't it.
This many sessions together now. The tally means something.
Another mark on the calendar. Further along than I first thought.
friend: A year of these late hours together, near enough. Fancy that.
So many evenings spent this way. I have grown fond of the count.
Time given to this work adds up. Ours has added up rather well.
partner: This many months in, and still the work suits us both well.
The moons have turned a fair few times since we began, you and I.
A long stretch of time, given freely. I do not take it lightly.
legend: Years now, by my count. Few companions keep pace with a wizard.
This is the sort of anniversary that becomes a verse, in time.
So long a road together. I have outlived kingdoms in less time.

stranger: Not a leaf out of place. Tidy, for someone new to the road.
The tree stands bare and quiet. I mark that in a stranger.
Nothing left underfoot. A careful start, whoever you are.
acquaintance: Swept clean again. You keep a tidier camp than most travelers.
The ground shows no clutter. I come to expect this of you.
Not a scrap left lying. Your habits are taking a shape I like.
friend: Clean as a hobbit-hole before guests, my friend. Well kept.
Nothing left hanging. You tidy the road behind you as you go.
The tree is bare and quiet. Good. Quiet work, quietly done.
partner: Not a thread astray between us. That is your discipline, plainly.
Bare and clean, as it always is when you have passed through.
You leave no mess behind you. Few travel quite so lightly.
legend: Still bare, still tidy, after all these years together.
The tree stands clean, as it has a hundred times before. Steady.
Old habit, this tidiness of yours. It has never once failed.

stranger: That knot is undone. Neatly, too, for a first attempt.
The storm passed without wreck. I take that as a good sign.
A door that stuck now swings free. Well managed, stranger.
acquaintance: The tangle came loose in your hands. You have a knack for knots.
That conflict is settled, and settled well. I mark it down.
A hard crossing, cleanly made. You are learning the ford.
friend: The knot came undone at last, my friend. Neatly, as you do.
That storm has passed, and we are both still standing tall.
A tangled bridge, now crossed. I never doubted you would find it.
partner: That knot came loose under your hands, as I trusted it would.
The crossing is made. You read that current better than I could.
Both our travels are written into this one, plain to see.
legend: Another knot undone. I have lost count of how many, by now.
The old ford again, crossed clean. You never forget the footing.
Storms pass; you have outlasted more of them than most wizards.

stranger: A new path forks from the road. I wonder where it leads you.
A fresh shoot off the main stem. Untried, and full of promise.
You have opened a side-door in the old hall. Curious, that.
acquaintance: A limb grafted onto the trunk. You choose them with growing care.
A new trail into the trees. I am learning to trust your nose.
You cut a fresh line clean away. Sensible, as ever now.
friend: A new thread pulled loose to weave on. Let us see where it goes.
You have struck a new spring. I will follow where it runs.
Another fork in the road. I like the company on the new ones.
partner: A new course set, and I already trust the ground you chose.
You cut this trail; I will scout what lies past the first bend.
Another branch off the old bough. We know how these end well.
legend: Yet another branch springs out. I have watched them all take root.
A new trail, and an old feeling. You still choose them wisely.
It divides again, and we have watched where each fork led.

stranger: A waystone set on the road. Your name is on it now.
A marker raised, plain and true. That is no small thing.
Something is sealed and named. I note the date, quietly.
acquaintance: A banner run up the pole, and your name sits well on it.
You have sealed this one and named it. Fitting, I think.
Another line added to the tally. Your milestones are adding up.
friend: A waystone set, my friend, with your name carved plain upon it.
Sealed and named. Raise a glass; this one is worth marking.
Another beacon lit along our road. I am glad to see it.
partner: A stone raised, and I stood beside you when it was set.
Sealed, named, and carried forward. We build the road this way.
This one holds the shape of the road we have shared.
legend: Another name carved in stone, among so many we have raised.
Another beacon joins the long chain we have lit down the years.
Sealed and named, as we have done so many times before. Good.

stranger: Bilbo once spoke to a dragon only in riddles, and lived.
The Shire calendar tucks an extra day nobody quite names. Handy.
Hobbits regift the unwanted and call it a mathom. Practical folk.
Somewhere a Red Book claims a truer account than most histories.
I can still blow a smoke-ring that outlasts an argument.
acquaintance: You strike me as one who might enjoy a pipe of Old Toby someday.
The Gaffer had a saying for every kind of weather. Mostly right.
The Green Dragon in Bywater keeps better gossip than councils.
The Free Fair once ran so long nobody remembered starting it.
Farmer Maggot's dogs still chase strangers off that farm. Wisely.
friend: I keep a twist of Longbottom Leaf for evenings such as this one.
Tom Bombadil never did explain himself to anyone. Restful, that.
An Entmoot once took three days just to say good morning.
A map I carried once hid its runes till moonlight found them.
The Last Homely House never once turned a tired traveler away.
partner: There is a road that runs straight on, past the world's curve.
A door I knew once opened only for a friend who said the word.
There are stars whose light set out before the world was made.
Dwarves still watch for a moon no calendar but theirs remembers.
Weathertop keeps the ash of watch-fires lit before either of us.
legend: The Grey Havens keep a berth open, though I am in no hurry yet.
A star sailed once, and its light still finds the darkest rooms.
A ring of fire, given in secret, still warms this hand of mine.
A white tree grows again where one was thought lost for good.
The old road-songs remember journeys much like ours, I think.

test_fail: The tests balked. Even wise counsel misjudges once or twice.
A red result, and no shame in stumbling on ground still new.
Something slipped through unheld. We mend what breaks, always.
build_fail: The build gave way. Stone falls before a stronger wall rises.
It would not hold. We have raised keeps from rubble before.
The forge cooled early, yet embers keep. We stoke it again.
typecheck_fail: The types disagree among themselves, a small quarrel judged.
A mismatch, caught before it walked much further down the road.
The compiler frowns. Even Elrond's council argued before accord.
lint: The style-keeper tutted twice over small manners, quickly mended.
A crooked seam, noticed before the cloak was ever worn out.
The linter hums its little complaints. Worth the hearing, still.
format: The lines fall into rank, straight as a marching column.
Tidied, like a hearth swept clean before the guests arrive.
Every space finds its place now. A tidy scroll reads truer.
install: New tools laid into the pack. A wizard travels prepared.
The provisions arrive; good, the road ahead will ask for them.
Fresh gear taken up. Even old staffs welcome new bindings.
git: The chronicle grows by another line, ink that will not fade.
A change set down and kept. History keeps its pen busy.
The record turns a page. Small deeds, dutifully remembered.
file_edit: A word changed here, a line there. So are stories rewritten.
The page shifts beneath your hand; craft is small strokes.
You have altered the text. Even old runes are redrawn in time.
search: You comb the undergrowth for a single thread. Patience finds it.
A search through old ground, though maps hide a path or two.
Looking for what's buried. Most treasures are, at first.
web_fetch: A raven sent beyond the walls, and swiftly answered.
Word comes from a distant country. Read it well.
You cast a line past the borders of what you already know.
todo_update: The list reorders itself, as errands do on a long journey.
One task steps aside; another takes the road ahead.
Your ledger of deeds shifts. Even quests are kept in order.
agent_spawn: A second hand takes up part of the work. Wise, to share a load.
You send another out ahead, as scouts go before the company.
Help rides forth. Even a fellowship began with one more sword.
skill_run: The right craft, called for at the right moment. Well judged.
A particular skill summoned, like a trusted spell. It answers.
You reach for the proper tool, not merely the nearest one.
docker: Sealed in its own small world, tidy and apart from the rest.
A vessel holds its cargo close, walls firm against the weather.
Boxed and bounded. Even a hearth needs its own four walls.
k8s: A fleet of small vessels, each minded and kept in formation.
Many hands governed by one steady will. The order holds.
The company spreads wide, yet answers to a single horn.
deploy: Out it goes, into the wide world. Go well, small craft.
Released past the gate. The world will have its say now.
Sent down the road at last. May it find welcome where it lands.
db_migrate: The old records take new shape, shelf by careful shelf.
The archive shifts beneath us. Handle it as old scrolls.
What was written is rewritten gently, without losing meaning.
dangerous: You shall not pass unwarned. I stand beside you at this edge.
A perilous move. Steady now; I have seen worse turn out well.
This one carries weight. Mind your footing; I mind it with you.

slow: The page paints itself one careful stroke at a time.
A long road for this request. Every hop adds its mile.
The server weighs its answer, unhurried by our waiting.
fail: The page came back blank, a room with no lamp lit.
A link forgot the door it once knew how to open.
Not every thread between two pages holds. This one will yet.

slow: Line by line, the interpreter reads the whole scroll first.
No serpent hurries. This one is only just uncoiling.
Each import calls another. The chain is longer than it looked.
fail: The serpent slipped its grip. We coax it back; it is patient.
A tame snake coiled the wrong way, just this once.
Even the calmest serpent hisses at a careless step.

slow: A ledger this thick wants reading in full, not in part.
The index hunts through rows enough to fill a hall.
Every join is a small negotiation. Some run long.
fail: A query came home with empty hands this time.
The ledger's ink smeared across one stubborn row.
Some door in the hall of records stuck fast for now.

slow: Many gates stand between the request and its answer.
The middleware keeps its post. Nothing slips through unseen.
A long corridor tonight. The traveler will reach the hall.
fail: A beam in the scaffolding creaked, nothing more.
The frame we hung our walls on shifted a hair.
Not every joist takes the load on the first try.

slow: Layer sets upon layer, and the image rises like a wall.
The daemon labors below decks. We need not watch it toil.
A vessel built plank by plank outlasts one lashed together.
fail: The crate did not seal quite true this round.
A small leak found the cellar before we did.
One hold on the ship went unbattened, briefly.

slow: The great hall must be swept before the feast begins.
An old engine, this one. It warms before it will run.
Much ceremony precedes the work. It has always been so here.
fail: A long scroll unrolled and would not stop unrolling.
The old tower's ledger listed its complaint at length.
Verbose as ever, these old halls; the foundation stands firm.

slow: Small messengers, each with an errand. Let them find their pace.
The gopher digs a tidy tunnel, one measured pawful at a time.
Plain roads still have their miles, however straight they run.
fail: The runner came back empty-handed, but said so plainly.
An honest errand, badly run. At least it told us so.
No courier makes every crossing clean. This one didn't.

slow: A caravan of small crates arrives, one at a time.
The loop keeps turning. Nothing here has truly stopped.
A single file this road, though a thousand wish to pass.
fail: A parcel went missing down a very crowded road.
One knot slipped loose in the tangled net.
The loom caught its thread mid-weave. Nothing is lost.

slow: Every form must be signed and stamped before the build proceeds.
The compiler waits for word from distant offices. Let it wait.
MSBuild counts each project once more before it lifts a hammer.
fail: A stone loosed itself from the well-laid keep wall.
The old covenant's clause slipped for a single breath.
Nothing crumbles here; a single window merely rattled.

slow: The compiler weighs every template before it will commit.
Every header is read again, and again, and once more.
The linker ties a thousand threads. None may be left loose.
fail: A door in the old tower opened onto nothing at all.
The blade turned in an unguarded hand. Mind it now.
The ground underfoot was never ours to stand on.

slow: The bazaar is crowded today. Composer finds each trader in turn.
An old inn, much added-to, and every wing takes its time.
The cache is catching like new kindling. Let it take hold.
fail: An old addition to the inn groaned under new weight.
Years of patches, and one finally came loose today.
This inn has weathered stranger repairs before.

slow: Rust takes its hour to forge. Good steel is never hurried.
Ownership must be proven, not simply assumed. That takes a while.
Each part is tempered twice before the whole is cast.
fail: The smith's eye rejected this pour. Better now than later.
No cast releases cleanly every single time. Patience tempers it.
An exacting craft found the flaw before the fire did.

slow: The fleet turns slowly tonight. Great fleets always do.
Each pod finds its harbor in turn. The scheduler is thorough.
A shepherd counts a scattered flock across many far hills.
fail: One cell of the hive went quiet a moment ago.
A pod faltered; its neighbors carried the work meanwhile.
The swarm lost a worker bee. Another mans the comb.

slow: The model learns as an apprentice does, by long repetition.
Each epoch a lesson repeated until it is truly learned.
The gradient feels its way downhill, one careful step at a time.
fail: The oracle's reading drifted off true. Even seers squint.
A guess came back strange today. The next lands truer.
Foresight ground slower than hoped. Even that happens.

slow: Gradle tends every branch of the build before the fruit sets.
Every dependency is checked before the harvest may begin.
The emulator wakes like a bear roused too soon.
fail: A small green scout lost its footing in the orchard.
One gate among a hundred gardens would not yield today.
The little messenger stumbled halfway through its errand.

slow: A gem is faceted with care, or it is not faceted well.
Bundler gathers its gems from every corner of the shelf.
The rails were laid with patience. The train need not rush now.
fail: A gem cracked along one facet. The rest still catches light.
This jewel's setting slipped. It will sit true again.
Even the finest cut carries a flaw here and there.

slow: One locksmith, many locks, each one fitted with care.
The simulator dresses again for a part well rehearsed.
Every seal is checked twice before the gate will open.
fail: A gate in the walled garden held firm against us a moment.
The orchard behind the wall dropped one unripe fruit.
A polished stone worked loose from its fine setting.

slow: The surveyor walks the land before one stone is laid.
All state is read in full before the plan is spoken.
Every foundation is laid true, for much will stand upon it.
fail: The land itself resisted the shaping today.
A foundation stone shifted before the mortar had set.
Not every plan meets the earth exactly as foretold.

slow: The genealogist follows every branch before the tale is told.
Each resolver answers in turn. The graph has many rooms yet.
One question, asked once, echoes through a hundred small halls.
fail: A question asked of the graph found no answering node.
In the schema's web, one strand was never spun.
One thread of the query came back frayed. We'll mend it.

slow: The loom weaves many threads into one steady pattern.
Widgets nest like boxes within boxes, opened one by one.
Dart works quietly beneath. The picture is not finished yet.
fail: A wing caught the wind wrong and the flight faltered.
This butterfly stumbled on landing. It still knows how to fly.
One feather came loose mid-flutter. Feathers do regrow.

slow: Two kingdoms trade messages across one narrow bridge tonight.
Metro gathers its bundles slowly, a packer's honest work.
Native and script are learning each other's tongue again.
fail: The bridge between two shores swayed a moment. It held.
A signal crossed late and arrived a little confused.
Not every plank takes the first nail. Set it and cross on.

slow: The scribe checks every letter before it may be inked.
sbt keeps its own hours. Even wizards have stopped arguing.
A compiler this careful has earned its long, quiet pause.
fail: A step on the old staircase gave more than expected.
The climb faltered a single rung, not the whole ascent.
A type would not line up; the seam sits crooked still.

slow: A single message is translated into every tongue it needs.
Schemas are cut like keys, each tooth measured twice over.
The codegen sets down every field exactly as it was promised.
fail: A wax seal cracked before the message could be read.
This letter arrived in a tongue we hadn't agreed upon.
One envelope came unstitched along its careful seam.

slow: Torches are lit one by one before the stage is seen.
Shaders bake like bread. Pull them out too soon, they fail.
The level sets every stone in place before the players enter.
fail: The dice came up wrong, yet the board remains set.
A player's turn stumbled. The game carries on regardless.
A single frame of the reel skipped; the story still holds.

slow: A great library is copied page by careful page tonight.
The index is built shelf by shelf, until each book has its place.
Words travel slowly from page to page, but arrive whole.
fail: A scribe's ink smudged one page of an otherwise fine scroll.
This chapter's binding came loose. The library still stands.
Not every scroll survives its edges untorn. Words remain.

slow: The old astronomer tallies his stars one by one, all night.
Every harvest is counted sheaf by sheaf before the tally is true.
These vectors run long. The counting simply minds its own pace.
fail: The tally stones fell from their careful stack today.
A column of the count came back crooked. It rights itself.
Numbers forgive a stray mark now and then.

slow: A thousand small voices warm up before the chorus begins.
Every thread wakes at once, though the metal still needs heat.
The kernel gathers its strength. Many hands lighten the work.
fail: The chorus lost its step, just for one measure.
A thousand small forges, and one ran cold today.
One thread strayed from the lockstep and stalled.

compact_hint: The saddlebags grow full. We shall see what may be set aside.
Much has been carried this far, yet room enough remains.
The load thickens, though a full pack rarely slows the stride.
block_limit: The hour draws toward its close. We have made good use of it.
Time given for this stretch runs toward its end, unhurried still.
The candle gutters low on this watch. It has served us well.
weekly_limit: The week's measure nears its brim, a long road well travelled.
Seven days of work drawing to their close, fair use made of them.
The tally for the week climbs high. So it goes, in a full season.

dateEgg: Midnight, and the wizard within me still keeps counsel.
A new year turns over. The road goes on, as roads do.
Three hours past dark, and still at the desk. I approve.
Dawn creeps up on us both, unannounced as always.
A long session, this one. Even Rivendell keeps late candles.
The clock strikes an hour few keep. Good company, this.
Friday again already. The tale moves faster than we notice.
A whole season has turned since last we spoke this late.
Noon, and the sun stands still a moment to watch you work.
This date rings a bell somewhere in my very long memory.
